steps.title: Identify the mixed effects
steps.content: The text says recommender systems affected consumer behavior and increased sales. It also says the effects differed by product type: utilitarian products saw stronger effects on item views, while hedonic products saw stronger effects on likelihood of purchase.

steps.title: Reject overreach
steps.content: Choice B adds an unsupported claim about limiting variety. Choice C says recommender systems boost utilitarian visibility because those items are otherwise overlooked, which the text does not say. Choice D overstates the purchase effect as likely limited to hedonic items.

steps.title: Choose the supported statement
steps.content: The remaining statement captures both points: recommender systems can affect behavior, but the nature and strength of those effects vary across product categories. The answer is A.
